# ![](https://tree-portraits-pgp.familysearchcdn.org/ut4tn/thumb200s.jpg)               James Drayton Mink         Male       23 February 1871–26 May 1953    •    KJ5H-HLM                                   [Email](mailto:?subject=See%20the%20exciting%20information%20I%20found%20for%20James%20Drayton%20Mink.&body=https%3A%2F%2Fancestors.familysearch.org%2Fen%2FKJ5H-HLM%2Fjames-drayton-mink-1871-1953%3Fcid%3Dfs_email)

## Brief Life History of James Drayton

When James Drayton Mink was born on 23 February 1871, in Marion, Preston, Virginia, United States, his father, William Hiram Mink, was 32 and his mother, Amanda Melvina Perkins, was 33. He married Rebecca Parzada Perkins on 26 February 1891, in Rugby, Grayson, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Council Election Precinct, Adams, Idaho, United States in 1940 and Washington, Idaho, United States in 1950. He died on 26 May 1953, in Weiser, Washington, Idaho, United States, at the age of 82, and was buried in Weiser, Washington, Idaho, United States.

## Name Meaning

Mink

James

Drayton

Czech: from a shortened form of the personal name _Dominik_ (see Dominic ).

North German, Danish, and Dutch: from a pet form of the personal name Mein .

German: variant of Minke 2.

Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

**Possible Related Names**

Dominic

Minke

Minck

### 1872 · The First National Park

Age 1

Yellowstone National Park was given the title of the first national park by the U.S. Congress and signed into law by President Ulysses S. Grant. It is also believed to be the first national park in the world.

### 1896 · Plessy vs. Ferguson

Age 25

A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.
